candidates.idea_description: This extends NeRF to model dynamic scenes with both visual and audio properties. It learns a 4D (3D space + time) radiance field where each point also has a latent acoustic impedance or directivity parameter, allowing for photorealistic rendering from novel viewpoints *and* plausible spatial audio synthesis for any virtual microphone placement.
candidates.key_innovation: Unifies visual and acoustic scene properties within a single generative 3D representation, enabling consistent cross-modal editing and simulation.
candidates.addressed_gap: SoundSpaces provides simulated audio for static 3D environments; this creates a unified, dynamic representation for both visual and acoustic novel view synthesis, closing the sim-to-real gap for both modalities.

candidates.idea_title: Weakly Supervised Spatial Audio Source Separation via Visual Bounding Boxes
candidates.idea_description: This technique uses object bounding boxes from visual tracking as weak supervision to separate overlapping sound sources in a mixture. A model is trained to assign individual audio components to tracked objects, enabling isolated listening to a specific source in a cluttered acoustic scene, even when sources overlap spatially.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses visual object detectors as a proxy for audio source separation masks, bypassing the need for isolated source recordings during training.
candidates.addressed_gap: SELDnet detects overlapping sources but does not separate them; this provides a way to isolate and attribute sound components to individual objects for deeper scene understanding.

candidates.idea_title: Audio-Informed Occlusion Reasoning for Egocentric 3D Object Tracking
candidates.idea_description: Develop a training-free method that uses spatial audio direction-of-arrival (DoA) from SELDnet to infer positions of visually occluded objects in egocentric videos, integrated with ORB-SLAM2's mapping to maintain consistent global tracks. This extends SAVVY's stabilization to handle partial visibility, leveraging audio cues when vision fails.
candidates.key_innovation: Using audio DoA to resolve occlusions in dynamic scenes without training, directly bridging spatial audio localization with visual mapping.
candidates.addressed_gap: ORB-SLAM2 lacks audio integration for occlusion handling, and Look, Listen and Learn assumes full visibility; this addresses robustness in visually challenging egocentric scenarios.

candidates.idea_title: Semantic Audio Embedding for Egocentric Object Disambiguation in Instruction-Tuned Models
candidates.idea_description: Extend LLaVA-style visual instruction tuning to incorporate semantic audio features (e.g., speech content, object-specific sounds) for disambiguating similar-looking objects in egocentric spatial queries. This builds on AudioGPT's audio understanding to enhance AV-LLM reasoning in SAVVY.
candidates.key_innovation: Leveraging rich audio semantics beyond DoA for object identity and context, enabling finer-grained spatial reasoning in instruction-following models.
candidates.addressed_gap: AudioGPT focuses on audio generation/understanding without spatial grounding, and LLaVA ignores audio semantics; this addresses the need for multimodal object disambiguation in dynamic scenes.

candidates.idea_title: Self-Supervised Audio-Visual Alignment for Noisy Egocentric Recordings
candidates.idea_description: Create an algorithm that automatically aligns audio and visual streams in egocentric videos without perfect synchronization, using contrastive learning inspired by Look, Listen and Learn. This improves SAVVY's pipeline for real-world data where synchronization is imperfect.
candidates.key_innovation: Handling real-world noise and temporal misalignment through self-supervised correspondence, making audio-visual fusion more robust.
candidates.addressed_gap: Look, Listen and Learn and SAVVY assume synchronized data; this addresses the gap in applying the approach to casual egocentric recordings with variable quality.

candidates.idea_title: Proactive Audio Sampling for Efficient Egocentric Spatial Map Construction
candidates.idea_description: Design a strategy that dynamically samples audio based on visual uncertainty in egocentric video, optimizing when and where to use spatial audio cues from SELDnet for track estimation. This reduces computational load in SAVVY's Dynamic Global Map Construction.
candidates.key_innovation: Adaptive audio resource allocation guided by visual confidence, improving efficiency without sacrificing accuracy.
candidates.addressed_gap: SELDnet and SAVVY process audio uniformly; this addresses the opportunity to prioritize audio in contexts where it adds most value, enhancing real-time performance.

candidates.idea_title: Real-Time Audio-Visual SLAM with Dynamic Sound Source Mapping
candidates.idea_description: Integrate ORB-SLAM2 with continuous spatial audio DoA estimation to create a SLAM system that maps both visual features and sounding objects in 3D, updating in real-time for egocentric agents. This extends SAVVY's global map to include audio source trajectories.
candidates.key_innovation: Fully unifying audio and vision within a SLAM framework for dynamic environments, enabling richer spatial representations.
candidates.addressed_gap: ORB-SLAM2 is vision-only, and SAVVY separates audio processing; this addresses the need for a cohesive system that jointly reasons about visual and auditory spatial cues.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Modal Consistency Verification for Training-Free Egocentric Pipelines
candidates.idea_description: Develop a module that checks consistency between audio-derived and visual-derived object tracks in SAVVY, using principles from SoundSpaces' physically grounded audio to automatically correct discrepancies in the global map.
candidates.key_innovation: Automated error detection and correction via cross-modal validation, enhancing reliability without additional training.
candidates.addressed_gap: SAVVY assumes audio and vision are complementary; this addresses potential conflicts or noise by introducing a verification step inspired by 3D environmental constraints.

candidates.idea_title: Instruction-Tuned Audio-Visual Question Answering for Complex Spatial Queries
candidates.idea_description: Build an AV-LLM that answers nuanced spatial questions (e.g., 'What object was to my left when I heard a crash?') by grounding in synchronized audio-visual data from Ego4D-style videos, using instruction tuning from LLaVA and audio reasoning from AudioGPT.
candidates.key_innovation: Fine-grained temporal and spatial grounding through natural language queries that require joint audio-visual interpretation.
candidates.addressed_gap: Ego4D focuses on visual tasks, and SAVVY handles basic queries; this addresses the gap in supporting complex, temporally anchored questions that leverage audio events.

candidates.idea_title: Egocentric Audio-Visual Forecasting for Anticipatory Spatial Reasoning
candidates.idea_description: Predict future positions and states of objects in egocentric scenes by analyzing current audio-visual streams, using models that learn from SoundSpaces' simulated dynamics to forecast in real-world videos. This could enable proactive interactions in AR/VR applications.
candidates.key_innovation: Using audio-visual cues for spatio-temporal prediction, moving beyond passive tracking to active forecasting.
candidates.addressed_gap: SoundSpaces and SAVVY focus on current state estimation; this addresses the opportunity for predictive reasoning, which is underexplored in egocentric settings.

candidates.idea_title: Multi-Agent Egocentric Spatial Reasoning with Shared Audio Cues
candidates.idea_description: Develop a system where multiple egocentric agents (e.g., from different perspectives) share spatial audio information to collaboratively map and reason about a common environment, inspired by SoundSpaces' 3D audio platform. This extends SAVVY's single-agent pipeline to multi-agent scenarios.
candidates.key_innovation: Leveraging distributed audio-visual data for collaborative spatial understanding, enhancing coverage and accuracy.
candidates.addressed_gap: All predecessors are single-agent focused; this addresses the gap in multi-agent egocentric reasoning, which is relevant for robotics and social VR.

candidates.idea_title: Synthetic-to-Real Transfer for Egocentric Spatial Audio Datasets
candidates.idea_description: Generate large-scale, realistic egocentric audio-visual datasets using physics-based simulations from SoundSpaces, and use them to pre-train models for fine-tuning on real videos from Ego4D. This improves generalization of audio-visual learning in SAVVY-like systems.
candidates.addressed_gap: SoundSpaces is synthetic, and Ego4D lacks rich spatial audio; this addresses the scarcity of real-world datasets with synchronized, physically grounded audio.

candidates.idea_title: Inferring Object Material Properties from Spatial Audio Cues for Enhanced Egocentric Understanding
candidates.idea_description: This idea leverages spatial audio characteristics, such as resonance and echo patterns, to infer physical properties like material and size of sounding objects in egocentric scenes. By integrating these inferences with visual data from SAVVY's pipeline, the system can provide richer object representations for 3D reasoning.
candidates.key_innovation: Moves beyond mere localization to derive deeper semantic attributes from audio, enabling more comprehensive object understanding without additional sensors.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors focus on audio-visual correspondence for localization but neglect using sound to infer object properties, limiting scene comprehension.

candidates.idea_title: Self-Supervised Acoustic Environment Adaptation Using Geometric Consistency from Egocentric Mapping
candidates.idea_description: Develop a method where spatial audio models, inspired by SELDnet, adapt to new acoustic environments (e.g., reverberant rooms) using unlabeled video data and geometric constraints from ORB-SLAM2-style mapping. This allows robust audio processing without environment-specific labeled data.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines unsupervised domain adaptation for audio with visual SLAM geometry to handle diverse real-world settings, reducing reliance on curated training data.
candidates.addressed_gap: Fixed spatial audio models from predecessors may not generalize across varying acoustic environments, limiting real-world applicability.

candidates.idea_title: Multi-Modal Fusion with Haptic Feedback for Robust Egocentric Spatial Mapping in AR/VR
candidates.idea_description: Extend SAVVY's audio-visual pipeline to incorporate haptic or tactile feedback, such as vibration patterns from wearable devices, to enhance egocentric mapping in low-visibility or noisy conditions. This fusion improves object tracking and spatial awareness in immersive applications.
candidates.key_innovation: Introduces haptics as an additional modality for cross-modal validation, offering redundancy and improving robustness where audio or vision fails.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors rely solely on audio and vision, which can be unreliable in scenarios with occlusions, noise, or poor lighting.

candidates.idea_title: Generative Audio Inpainting to Enable Spatial Reasoning in Silent Egocentric Videos
candidates.idea_description: Use generative models, building on AudioGPT, to simulate plausible spatial audio cues for silent video segments, allowing systems like SAVVY to function even when audio is absent or corrupted. This involves predicting sound direction and event timing based on visual context.
candidates.key_innovation: Applies audio generation techniques to fill in missing modality data, enabling consistent spatial reasoning without requiring actual audio input.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current methods fail when audio is unavailable, restricting their use to fully synchronized audio-visual datasets.

candidates.idea_title: Causal Reasoning Framework for Predicting Future Positions of Sounding Objects Using AV-LLMs
candidates.idea_description: Develop a causal inference module within AV-LLMs to predict the future trajectories of sounding objects based on historical audio-visual dynamics and scene context. This leverages instruction tuning from LLaVA and audio understanding from AudioGPT for proactive reasoning.
candidates.key_innovation: Shifts from reactive tracking to predictive modeling, enabling anticipatory planning and interaction in dynamic egocentric scenes.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors focus on current state estimation but lack the ability to forecast object behavior, which is crucial for real-time decision-making.

candidates.idea_title: Benchmark for Egocentric Spatial Reasoning in Extreme Environments with Synthetic Spatial Audio
candidates.idea_description: Create a new benchmark inspired by SoundSpaces and Ego4D, featuring scenarios like underwater or outer space, with synthetic spatial audio generated to mimic challenging acoustic conditions. This tests system generalization and robustness in non-standard settings.
candidates.key_innovation: Provides a controlled evaluation platform for extreme environments using generated data, addressing a gap in existing benchmarks that focus on typical indoor/outdoor scenes.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current benchmarks do not cover diverse or harsh environments, limiting the understanding of system performance in real-world edge cases.

candidates.idea_title: Multi-Agent Collaborative Egocentric Mapping Using Synchronized Audio for Global Consensus
candidates.idea_description: Implement a distributed mapping system where multiple egocentric devices (e.g., from AR glasses) share and fuse spatial audio-visual data to build a unified global map. This extends ORB-SLAM2's principles with audio synchronization for cross-device consistency.
candidates.key_innovation: Enables collaborative spatial understanding across devices, improving coverage and accuracy through shared auditory cues, which is novel compared to single-device approaches.
candidates.addressed_gap: Single-device mapping in predecessors may suffer from limited viewpoint or scale, hindering comprehensive scene reconstruction.

candidates.idea_title: Adversarial Training for Robust Spatial Audio-Visual Models Against Real-World Noise and Interference
candidates.idea_description: Introduce adversarial training techniques to make models like SELDnet and AV-LLMs resilient to common audio noise (e.g., background chatter) and visual distortions, ensuring reliable performance in uncontrolled egocentric environments.
candidates.key_innovation: Explicitly targets model robustness through adversarial examples, enhancing deployability in noisy real-world scenarios where predecessors may degrade.
candidates.addressed_gap: Previous models are sensitive to noise and adversarial conditions, reducing reliability in practical applications.

candidates.idea_title: Meta-Learning for Rapid AV-LLM Adaptation to New Sounding Object Categories with Few Examples
candidates.idea_description: Apply meta-learning strategies to AV-LLMs, allowing them to quickly adapt to novel sounding objects or scenes using only a few audio-visual examples. This builds on instruction tuning from LLaVA and audio processing from AudioGPT for efficient few-shot learning.
candidates.key_innovation: Enables fast customization to new domains without extensive retraining, addressing the data inefficiency of current models.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessor models require large labeled datasets for new categories, hindering scalability and adaptability to emerging object types.

candidates.idea_title: Audio-Visual Semantic Segmentation to Track Silent Objects via Correlation with Sounding Sources
candidates.idea_description: Develop a method that uses spatial audio from sounding objects to assist in segmenting and tracking non-sounding objects in egocentric video, leveraging correlations like proximity or occlusion. This extends Look, Listen and Learn by propagating localization cues to silent entities.
candidates.key_innovation: Integrates audio cues to improve visual segmentation for silent objects, which are typically ignored in audio-based systems, enhancing overall scene understanding.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors focus on sounding objects, leaving silent objects unaddressed, which limits comprehensive egocentric spatial reasoning.

candidates.idea_title: Real-Time Audio-Augmented Visual SLAM for Egocentric Mapping
candidates.idea_description: Develop a SLAM system that integrates real-time spatial audio cues from SELDnet-like DoA estimation with visual mapping from ORB-SLAM2 to enhance object tracking in dynamic egocentric scenes. This fusion allows for more robust localization of sounding objects, especially in visually cluttered or occluded environments.
candidates.key_innovation: Seamless integration of spatial audio direction-of-arrival into a training-free visual SLAM framework, enabling audio-guided stabilization of egocentric tracks in real-time.
candidates.addressed_gap: ORB-SLAM2 relies solely on vision and struggles with dynamic sounding objects; adding audio cues addresses limitations in visual-only mapping for egocentric spatial reasoning.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Modal Audio-Visual Association for Novel Sound Source Localization
candidates.idea_description: Leverage the audio-visual correspondence learning from Look, Listen and Learn to localize and associate sound sources not seen during training in egocentric video. This enables detection and tracking of new objects by generalizing from synchronized audio-visual pairs without task-specific retraining.
candidates.key_innovation: Extending audio-visual learning to generalize across unseen sound categories, using cross-modal associations to identify novel sources in dynamic scenes.
candidates.addressed_gap: Existing methods like SAVVY are limited to known sound classes; this addresses scalability to unknown sounds and improves adaptability in real-world scenarios.

candidates.idea_title: Spatial Audio-Driven Immersive Navigation in Virtual Environments
candidates.idea_description: Implement an embodied navigation agent in SoundSpaces that uses synchronized spatial audio and egocentric visual cues to solve 3D spatial queries, integrating AV-LLMs from AudioGPT for training-free reasoning. This extends SoundSpaces to support instruction-driven navigation tasks without environment-specific training.
candidates.key_innovation: Combining physically-grounded spatial audio from SoundSpaces with AV-LLM-based reasoning for egocentric navigation, enabling dynamic task execution in virtual environments.
candidates.addressed_gap: SoundSpaces focuses on audio for 3D environments but lacks integration with instruction-following LLMs; this bridges the gap for embodied AI tasks requiring spatial reasoning.

candidates.idea_title: Multi-Agent Egocentric Spatial Awareness Using Shared Audio-Visual Maps
candidates.idea_description: Enable multiple egocentric agents to collaboratively build and update a global audio-visual map by fusing spatial audio and visual tracks, inspired by SAVVY's Dynamic Global Map Construction. This supports multi-agent coordination in dynamic scenes through shared spatial understanding.
candidates.key_innovation: Extending single-agent egocentric mapping to multi-agent settings with real-time audio-visual data sharing, improving collective spatial reasoning and task efficiency.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors like ORB-SLAM2 and SAVVY focus on single-agent scenarios; this addresses the need for collaborative egocentric reasoning in multi-agent applications.

candidates.idea_title: Audio Events as Temporal Anchors for Fine-Grained Video Grounding
candidates.idea_description: Use sound events detected via SELDnet to provide precise temporal anchors for grounding language queries in egocentric video, enhancing temporal precision in tasks from Ego4D. This method uses audio cues to disambiguate visual events and improve temporal grounding accuracy.
candidates.key_innovation: Leveraging audio events as reliable temporal markers to supplement visual cues, enabling finer-grained temporal localization in dynamic egocentric scenes.
candidates.addressed_gap: Ego4D highlights challenges in temporal grounding with visual-only data; audio events offer more precise timing for events, addressing a key limitation in egocentric video analysis.

candidates.idea_title: Low-Power Audio-Visual Processing for Wearable Egocentric Devices
candidates.idea_description: Optimize the SAVVY pipeline for energy efficiency on wearable devices by using techniques like sparse audio processing, model quantization, and hardware-aware pruning. This ensures real-time performance while balancing accuracy and power consumption for practical deployment.
candidates.key_innovation: Focusing on energy-efficient implementation of audio-visual fusion and AV-LLM reasoning, tailored for resource-constrained egocentric wearables.
candidates.addressed_gap: Existing research does not address energy constraints in egocentric systems; this targets real-world applicability and deployment on low-power devices.

candidates.idea_title: Adaptive Fusion of Audio and Visual Cues Based on Scene Context
candidates.idea_description: Develop a context-aware method to dynamically adjust the weighting of audio and visual information in SAVVY's pipeline based on factors like noise levels, occlusion, or scene complexity. This improves robustness by prioritizing the most reliable modality in varying conditions.
candidates.key_innovation: Introducing adaptive fusion that responds to real-time scene dynamics, enhancing the reliability of audio-visual integration in egocentric spatial reasoning.
candidates.addressed_gap: SAVVY uses a fixed fusion approach; adaptive methods can better handle diverse real-world conditions, such as noisy audio or poor lighting, which are common in egocentric scenarios.

candidates.idea_title: Privacy-Preserving Egocentric Audio-Visual Analysis
candidates.idea_description: Process spatial audio and visual data to extract spatial information for tasks like localization and tracking without storing or transmitting raw audio, using on-device processing and differential privacy techniques. This maintains user privacy while enabling egocentric spatial reasoning.
candidates.key_innovation: Incorporating privacy safeguards into audio-visual systems, ensuring that sensitive audio data is not exposed during analysis or storage.
candidates.addressed_gap: Privacy concerns are not addressed in predecessors like Ego4D or SAVVY; this adds critical security layers for real-world egocentric devices handling personal data.

candidates.idea_title: Generative AI for Synthesizing Realistic Spatial Audio Datasets
candidates.idea_description: Use generative models to create diverse and realistic spatial audio scenes that mimic real-world conditions, building on SoundSpaces' simulation platform. This generates high-fidelity data for training or benchmarking audio-visual systems, reducing reliance on costly real-data collection.
candidates.key_innovation: Applying generative AI to produce synthetic spatial audio with realistic acoustics, bridging the gap between simulated and real-world environments for egocentric tasks.
candidates.addressed_gap: SoundSpaces provides controlled simulations, but real-world variability is lacking; generative models address the sim-to-real gap and improve dataset diversity for robust training.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Domain Transfer of Audio-Visual Reasoning to New Environments
candidates.idea_description: Develop domain adaptation techniques to transfer learned audio-visual correspondence and spatial reasoning from one environment to another with minimal fine-tuning, enabling systems like SAVVY to generalize across different acoustic and visual settings. This uses unsupervised or few-shot methods to adapt models to new scenes.
candidates.key_innovation: Enabling robust cross-domain generalization for audio-visual egocentric systems, allowing them to operate effectively in unseen environments without retraining.
candidates.addressed_gap: Existing systems are often environment-specific; this addresses the need for adaptability to new contexts, which is crucial for real-world deployment of egocentric technologies.

target_title: PRIMT: Preference-based Reinforcement Learning with Multimodal Feedback and Trajectory Synthesis from Foundation Models

candidates.idea_title: Adaptive Preference Evaluator Selection via Meta-Learning
candidates.idea_description: Develop a meta-learning system that dynamically selects which foundation model (LLM, VLM, or ensemble) evaluates each trajectory comparison, based on task domain and trajectory characteristics. This would improve evaluation accuracy by routing comparisons to the most competent evaluator.
candidates.key_innovation: Introduces adaptive evaluator selection rather than fixed fusion, treating foundation model competencies as context-dependent and learnable.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the limitation of fixed neuro-symbolic fusion in PRIMT, which may underutilize evaluator strengths or overuse weak evaluators for certain comparison types.

candidates.idea_title: Foresight Trajectory Refinement via Self-Consistency Filtering
candidates.idea_description: Implement a filtering mechanism that generates multiple candidate foresight trajectories using foundation models and selects only those that are self-consistent across multiple evaluations (e.g., VLM description matches LLM reasoning about intent). This improves synthetic data quality for warm-starting.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses self-consistency across modalities as a proxy for trajectory quality, reducing distributional shift in model-generated data more robustly than single-pass generation.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the risk of low-quality or hallucinated trajectories in PRIMT's foresight generation, which could mislead early reward learning.

candidates.idea_title: Temporal Decomposition for Fine-Grained Preference Attribution
candidates.idea_description: Break trajectories into sub-goal segments using foundation model analysis, then learn separate reward models for each segment. This allows the system to attribute preferences to specific temporal components, improving credit assignment in long-horizon tasks.
candidates.key_innovation: Extends T-REX's ranking insight by decomposing trajectories into analyzable sub-goals using foundation models, enabling segment-level credit assignment.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the coarse credit assignment problem in long-horizon tasks even with ranked trajectories, as identified in both T-REX and PRIMT.

candidates.idea_title: Contrastive Multimodal Embedding for Preference Learning
candidates.idea_description: Learn a joint embedding space that aligns VLM visual features, LLM textual reasoning, and robot state trajectories. Preferences are then evaluated by comparing distances in this shared space, enabling more nuanced preference judgments than pairwise comparisons.
candidates.key_innovation: Moves beyond pairwise comparisons to embedding-based preference learning, allowing continuous similarity measures and potentially better generalization to novel trajectories.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the limitation of discrete pairwise evaluations in PRIMT, which may struggle with subtle differences between similar trajectories.

candidates.idea_title: Adversarial Robustness through Preference Consistency Verification
candidates.idea_description: Train a discriminator to detect when foundation model preferences are inconsistent or adversarial (e.g., due to visual ambiguity or LLM reasoning errors), and use this to weight or reject evaluations. This improves reliability in safety-critical scenarios.
candidates.key_innovation: Introduces adversarial robustness specifically for foundation model preferences, addressing known failure modes of VLMs and LLMs in embodied evaluation.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the single-modality brittleness and potential unreliability of foundation model evaluations, which PRIMT identifies but doesn't fully solve.

candidates.idea_title: Interactive Preference Learning with Foundation Model Explanations
candidates.idea_description: When the foundation model expresses high uncertainty, generate natural language explanations of its evaluation reasoning and present them to humans for targeted feedback. This reduces human effort while still resolving critical ambiguities.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines human-in-the-loop with foundation model explanations to create more efficient human feedback, focusing human attention where foundation models are weakest.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the residual need for human feedback in ambiguous cases, as identified in both Sadigh et al. and PRIMT, but makes human involvement more efficient and informative.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Embodiment Preference Transfer via Foundation Model Abstraction
candidates.idea_description: Use foundation models to abstract robot behaviors into high-level semantic descriptions (e.g., 'reaching toward the cup'), then transfer preferences learned on one robot morphology to another by matching these abstract descriptions.
candidates.key_innovation: Leverages foundation models' semantic understanding to enable cross-embodiment preference transfer, potentially reducing data needs for new robots.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the embodiment specificity of preference-based RL, which limits generalization across different robot platforms or morphologies.

candidates.idea_title: Dynamic Curriculum Generation for Reward Model Training
candidates.idea_description: Use foundation models to analyze the current reward model's weaknesses and generate synthetic trajectory comparisons that specifically target those weaknesses. This creates an adaptive curriculum that accelerates reward model refinement.
candidates.key_innovation: Applies curriculum learning principles to preference-based reward learning, with foundation models as both evaluators and curriculum designers.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the potential inefficiency of random or uniform trajectory sampling for reward model training, which may not focus on critical decision boundaries.

candidates.idea_title: Multimodal Preference Consensus as Reward Regularization
candidates.idea_description: Regularize the learned reward model by adding a loss term that encourages agreement between VLM evaluations, LLM evaluations, and the model's own predictions. This prevents overfitting to noisy or biased evaluations from any single modality.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses consensus across modalities as a regularizer rather than just as a decision mechanism, improving reward model robustness without requiring perfect agreement.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the challenge of combining potentially conflicting evaluations from different modalities in a principled way that improves overall model reliability.

candidates.idea_title: Self-Supervised Foundation Model Fine-tuning for Embodied Evaluation
candidates.idea_description: Fine-tune foundation models on automatically collected robot trajectory data with synthetic annotations (e.g., from reinforcement learning successes/failures), making them better at evaluating embodied behavior without human labels.
candidates.key_innovation: Improves foundation model evaluation capability for robotics through domain-specific fine-tuning, addressing the domain gap between web-trained models and embodied settings.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the limitation that general-purpose foundation models may not be optimally suited for nuanced evaluation of robotic behaviors, as acknowledged in PRIMT's use of them as-is.

candidates.idea_title: Dynamic Foundation Model Selection for Context-Aware Preference Evaluation
candidates.idea_description: Develop a meta-learning framework that dynamically selects or combines different foundation models (e.g., VLMs, LLMs) based on task or environment characteristics to optimize preference evaluation accuracy and efficiency. This system adapts in real-time to changing conditions, such as lighting or object types, to provide robust feedback.
candidates.key_innovation: Introduces adaptability in FM usage through context-driven model selection, moving beyond static fusion to a self-optimizing system that improves evaluation quality.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the limitation in PRIMT of using fixed FM fusion, which may not be optimal for all scenarios, leading to suboptimal performance in dynamic or novel environments.

candidates.idea_title: Causal Disentanglement in Foundation-Model-Generated Preferences
candidates.idea_description: Apply causal inference techniques to identify and mitigate spurious correlations in preferences generated by foundation models, such as confounding factors in visual or textual feedback. By intervening on causal variables, the system produces more reliable reward models that generalize better to unseen tasks.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses causal reasoning to improve the reliability of AI-generated preferences, moving beyond correlation-based learning to disentangle true performance indicators from noise.
candidates.addressed_gap: Tackles the potential biases and inaccuracies in FM evaluations from predecessors like Constitutional AI and LLM-as-a-Judge, where preferences may be influenced by irrelevant factors.

candidates.idea_title: Meta-Learning for Rapid Domain Adaptation of FM Evaluators
candidates.idea_description: Employ meta-learning algorithms to enable foundation model evaluators to quickly adapt to new tasks or domains with minimal data, such as transferring from household to industrial robotics. This reduces the need for extensive retraining and accelerates deployment in diverse settings.
candidates.key_innovation: Allows FM evaluators to learn how to learn, leveraging prior knowledge to adapt rapidly, which enhances generalization and reduces computational costs.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the high data and computational requirements of retraining foundation models for new environments, a gap in PRIMT and related work that relies on static FMs.

candidates.idea_title: Lightweight Human Feedback for Correcting AI-Generated Preferences
candidates.idea_description: Integrate a sparse human feedback loop where experts periodically review and correct a small subset of AI-generated preferences, using active learning to select the most informative cases. This hybrid system balances automation with human oversight, improving trust and accuracy without significant human effort.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines the efficiency of AI feedback with the precision of human judgment in a scalable, selective manner, creating a closed-loop refinement process.
candidates.addressed_gap: Mitigates the risk of AI feedback errors in systems like PRIMT, while maintaining the goal of reduced human involvement from predecessors like Christiano et al.

candidates.idea_title: Collaborative Preference Learning in Multi-Agent Robotic Systems
candidates.idea_description: Extend the PRIMT framework to multi-agent settings where multiple robots or agents share and jointly learn from foundation model evaluations, enabling collective improvement in sample efficiency and coordination. Agents can pool feedback to handle complex tasks that require teamwork.
candidates.key_innovation: Leverages collective intelligence from multiple agents and shared FM evaluations, introducing social learning dynamics to preference-based RL.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses scalability and sample efficiency in complex multi-agent tasks, a gap in prior work that focuses on single-agent scenarios.

candidates.idea_title: Hierarchical Temporal Abstraction for Improved Credit Assignment
candidates.idea_description: Incorporate hierarchical reinforcement learning concepts, such as options or skills, to evaluate trajectories at multiple temporal scales—from low-level actions to high-level goals. This enhances credit assignment for long-horizon tasks by allowing FM evaluations to provide feedback on different levels of behavior.
candidates.key_innovation: Integrates temporal abstraction with FM-based preference learning, enabling more nuanced and effective credit assignment over extended behaviors.
candidates.addressed_gap: Improves upon T-REX's credit assignment by adding hierarchical structure, addressing the challenge of long-horizon tasks in preference learning.

candidates.idea_title: Adversarial Training for Robust Foundation-Model-Based Rewards
candidates.idea_description: Develop adversarial training methods to harden FM-generated rewards against manipulation or distribution shifts, such as by generating adversarial trajectories that test reward model resilience. This ensures safe deployment in real-world environments where inputs may be noisy or deceptive.
candidates.key_innovation: Proactively addresses security and robustness issues in AI-driven reward systems through adversarial robustness techniques, a novel focus for preference learning.
candidates.addressed_gap: Focuses on safety and reliability, a critical gap in autonomous systems that use AI feedback, as seen in predecessors like Constitutional AI.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Modal Transfer Learning for Enhanced Preference Models
candidates.idea_description: Use transfer learning to leverage knowledge from one modality, such as text-based LLMs, to improve preference evaluation in another, like vision-based VLMs, for tasks where data is scarce. This cross-pollination enhances data efficiency and model performance across modalities.
candidates.key_innovation: Enables knowledge sharing across modalities, reducing the need for modality-specific data and improving the fusion process in multimodal systems.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the data scarcity in specific modalities for multimodal fusion, a challenge in PRIMT's integration of VLMs and LLMs.

candidates.idea_title: Uncertainty-Guided Foresight Trajectory Generation for Exploration
candidates.idea_description: Integrate uncertainty quantification into trajectory generation from foundation models, prioritizing the synthesis of trajectories where the model is uncertain to drive exploration in under-represented states. This improves sample efficiency and reduces the risk of overfitting to known regions.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses uncertainty to guide trajectory synthesis, making exploration more efficient and targeted, which advances MBPO-inspired methods.
candidates.addressed_gap: Enhances MBPO-inspired trajectory generation in PRIMT by adding exploration guidance, addressing the limitation of random or suboptimal model-generated rollouts.

candidates.idea_title: Embedding Ethical Guidelines in Foundation Model Preference Evaluation
candidates.idea_description: Incorporate explicit ethical or constitutional constraints, such as fairness and harm avoidance, into the FM evaluation process through constrained optimization or rule-based filters. This ensures that generated preferences adhere to predefined societal norms, enhancing alignment.
candidates.key_innovation: Directly integrates ethics into the preference learning loop via structured guidelines, going beyond implicit alignment in predecessors like Constitutional AI.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses alignment and safety concerns in AI feedback systems, ensuring that preferences are not only efficient but also ethically sound.

candidates.idea_title: Debiasing Foundation-Model-Generated Preferences for Fair Reward Learning
candidates.idea_description: Develop methods to identify and mitigate biases in the preferences generated by foundation models (LLMs and VLMs) to ensure the learned reward function does not encode societal biases or artifacts from pretraining data. This involves training debiasing adapters or using adversarial training to align model evaluations with fairness criteria.
candidates.key_innovation: Introduces bias detection and correction mechanisms specifically tailored for FM-generated preferences in RL, going beyond simple aggregation of AI feedback.
candidates.addressed_gap: The predecessors assume FM preferences are reliable, but FM biases can lead to unfair or incorrect reward functions.

candidates.idea_title: Hierarchical Reward Learning with Foundation Models for Long-Horizon Tasks
candidates.idea_description: Use foundation models to decompose long-horizon robotic tasks into subtasks and learn hierarchical reward functions that evaluate progress at multiple levels of abstraction. This involves prompting LLMs to extract subtask goals and VLMs to verify subgoal completion, enabling credit assignment over extended timelines.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines hierarchical reward learning with FM-driven subtask decomposition and evaluation, enabling credit assignment over extended timelines.
candidates.addressed_gap: T-REX and PRIMT focus on trajectory-level rankings, but long-horizon tasks require temporal abstraction.

candidates.idea_title: Adversarial Robustness of FM-Based Reward Models via Synthetic Perturbations
candidates.idea_description: Generate adversarial trajectories using foundation models to stress-test and improve the robustness of the reward model to distributional shifts and deceptive behaviors. This involves creating perturbations that maximize reward model disagreement and using them to augment training data.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses foundation models not only for generating trajectories but also for creating adversarial examples to harden the reward model.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current methods do not account for adversarial scenarios that could exploit weaknesses in the reward model.

candidates.idea_title: Multi-Agent Preference Learning with Foundation Models for Collaborative Tasks
candidates.idea_description: Extend FM-based preference learning to multi-agent settings where the reward function must evaluate joint behavior and coordination in collaborative tasks. This involves adapting neuro-symbolic fusion to evaluate interactions, such as teamwork quality or conflict resolution, from multi-view video and language inputs.
candidates.key_innovation: Adapts the neuro-symbolic fusion of LLMs and VLMs to evaluate interactions between agents, not just single-agent trajectories.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors focus on single-agent tasks; multi-agent collaboration requires new forms of preference evaluation.

candidates.idea_title: Uncertainty-Aware Active Preference Querying with Foundation Models
candidates.idea_description: Integrate uncertainty estimates from foundation models into active learning, so that the system queries human preferences only when the FM is uncertain, thereby maximizing label efficiency. This involves calibrating FM confidence scores and designing an acquisition function that balances exploration and exploitation.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines FM-generated preferences with FM-derived uncertainty to create a hybrid human-AI active learning loop.
candidates.addressed_gap: PRIMT uses FM to replace human labels entirely, but this hybrid approach could further reduce human effort by focusing on ambiguous cases.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Domain Transfer of Reward Functions Using Foundation Model Priors
candidates.idea_description: Use foundation models to facilitate the transfer of reward functions across different but related robotic tasks or environments by capturing shared semantics. This involves aligning reward representations via FM embeddings and fine-tuning on minimal target-domain data.
candidates.key_innovation: Leverages the broad knowledge in foundation models to align reward functions across domains without requiring task-specific human feedback.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current methods are task-specific and do not exploit the transferability of knowledge in foundation models.

candidates.idea_title: Physical Plausibility Constraints for FM-Generated Trajectories via Neuro-Symbolic Fusion
candidates.idea_description: Incorporate physical constraints (e.g., dynamics, kinematics) into the foundation model trajectory generation process to ensure generated trajectories are physically realistic and safe. This involves using symbolic physics engines or rule-based systems to filter or correct FM-generated trajectories before they are used for training.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines symbolic physics engines or rules with foundation model generation to produce trajectories that are both semantically meaningful and physically plausible.
candidates.addressed_gap: FM-generated trajectories may violate physical laws, leading to unrealistic training data.

candidates.idea_title: Explainable Preference Judgments via Foundation Model Rationales
candidates.idea_description: Train foundation models to not only provide preference judgments but also generate natural language rationales explaining why one trajectory is preferred, enhancing interpretability. This involves fine-tuning models with rationale-augmented datasets or using prompt engineering to elicit explanations.
candidates.key_innovation: Integrates explanation generation into the preference learning pipeline, making the reward model more transparent.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current methods are black-box; explanations can help debug and improve the reward model.

candidates.idea_title: Dynamic Reward Model Adaptation to Evolving Environments
candidates.idea_description: Develop a method where the reward model, trained with FM-generated preferences, adapts online to changes in the environment or task requirements. This involves using continuous FM evaluation to update the reward model in non-stationary settings, unlike static reward models.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses continuous FM evaluation to update the reward model in non-stationary settings, unlike static reward models.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors assume a fixed environment; real-world tasks often involve dynamic changes.

candidates.idea_title: Curriculum Generation for Reward Learning via Foundation Model Storytelling
candidates.idea_description: Use foundation models to generate a sequence of increasingly challenging tasks or scenarios (a curriculum) that facilitate efficient reward model learning. This involves prompting LLMs to design task progressions and VLMs to synthesize corresponding demonstrations or evaluation criteria.
candidates.key_innovation: Applies the idea of curriculum learning to preference-based RL by having FMs design the training progression.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current methods do not systematically order the learning tasks; a curriculum can accelerate learning and improve final performance.

candidates.idea_title: Adaptive Modality Fusion for Robust Multimodal Feedback
candidates.idea_description: Develop a neuro-symbolic framework that dynamically adjusts the weighting between LLM and VLM inputs based on task complexity and modality reliability, using a meta-learning approach to optimize fusion parameters. This allows the system to handle conflicting or ambiguous signals more effectively during preference evaluation.
candidates.key_innovation: Moves beyond static fusion to adaptive weighting, reducing conflicts and improving evaluation accuracy in diverse scenarios by learning optimal fusion strategies from data.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the brittleness of single-modality or fixed-fusion methods in handling ambiguous or conflicting signals, as identified in predecessors like LLM-as-a-Judge and SayCan.

candidates.idea_title: Adversarial Training for Calibrated Foundation Model Evaluators
candidates.idea_description: Use adversarial examples to fine-tune foundation models as evaluators, ensuring their preferences are calibrated against human judgments and resistant to manipulation. This involves generating challenging trajectory pairs that expose model biases and iteratively correcting them.
candidates.key_innovation: Introduces adversarial robustness to AI feedback systems, enhancing reliability and safety by actively testing and improving model calibration.
candidates.addressed_gap: Tackles potential biases or overconfidence in foundation model evaluations, which could lead to misaligned rewards, building on concerns from Constitutional AI and LLM-as-a-Judge.

candidates.idea_title: Human-AI Collaborative Preference Elicitation
candidates.idea_description: Design an active learning system where foundation models propose preference queries, but humans intervene only when uncertainty is high, using a Bayesian approach to quantify model confidence. This hybrid method strategically involves humans for critical decisions while maximizing AI efficiency.
candidates.key_innovation: Integrates human oversight strategically, combining the efficiency of AI feedback with human expertise for high-stakes or ambiguous evaluations, optimizing the human-AI loop.
candidates.addressed_gap: Reduces human effort while ensuring reliability, addressing the label burden and ambiguity issues from Active Preference-Based Learning by leveraging AI for routine cases.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Domain Reward Transfer via Foundation Model Embeddings
candidates.idea_description: Learn a shared embedding space using foundation models to transfer reward models across different but related tasks, enabling quick adaptation to new environments. This involves aligning trajectory features from source and target domains using foundation model representations.
candidates.key_innovation: Leverages foundation model generalization to transfer preferences, reducing data requirements for new tasks and enabling few-shot reward learning.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the cold-start problem in preference learning by reusing knowledge from similar tasks, extending the efficiency gains from T-REX and PRIMT.

candidates.idea_title: Self-Refining Synthetic Trajectories via Policy Feedback
candidates.idea_description: Implement a closed-loop system where the policy's performance is used to refine the foundation model's trajectory generation, improving realism and relevance over time. This uses reinforcement learning to adjust generation parameters based on policy outcomes.
candidates.key_innovation: Creates a feedback loop between policy learning and trajectory synthesis, optimizing synthetic data generation to better match the agent's evolving capabilities.
candidates.addressed_gap: Mitigates distribution shift issues in synthetic trajectories, as seen in MBPO-style approaches, by adapting them dynamically to avoid artifacts.

candidates.idea_title: Uncertainty-Aware Multimodal Preference Aggregation
candidates.idea_description: Develop methods to quantify uncertainty in each modality's evaluations and use probabilistic models to aggregate preferences, guiding exploration based on confidence levels. This involves Bayesian deep learning to estimate uncertainty in VLM and LLM outputs.
candidates.key_innovation: Incorporates uncertainty quantification into preference learning, enabling more informed decision-making and targeted exploration to resolve ambiguities.
candidates.addressed_gap: Improves credit assignment and sample efficiency by focusing on high-uncertainty regions, enhancing the robustness of PRIMT's multimodal evaluation.

candidates.idea_title: Constitutional Rules for Embodied AI Feedback
candidates.idea_description: Embed ethical and safety constraints directly into the foundation models' evaluation process through rule-based layers, ensuring generated preferences adhere to predefined guidelines. This uses symbolic reasoning to enforce rules during VLM+LLM fusion.
candidates.key_innovation: Directly integrates constitutional AI principles into multimodal feedback for robotics, enhancing safety and alignment with human values in embodied settings.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses ethical concerns in AI-generated preferences by enforcing explicit rules during evaluation, building on Constitutional AI but applied to robotics.

candidates.idea_title: Efficient Preference Learning via Model-Based Caching
candidates.idea_description: Use similarity search on foundation model embeddings to cache past evaluations and quickly retrieve preferences for similar trajectory pairs, reducing query costs. This involves indexing trajectories using foundation model features for fast retrieval.
candidates.key_innovation: Applies caching techniques from information retrieval to preference learning, improving computational efficiency by avoiding redundant foundation model queries.
candidates.addressed_gap: Reduces the expense of repeatedly querying large foundation models, addressing scalability issues in LLM-as-a-Judge and PRIMT systems.

candidates.idea_title: Foundation Model-Informed Model Predictive Control
candidates.idea_description: Integrate foundation models with model predictive control by using them to generate candidate trajectories for MPC, combining long-horizon planning with learned preferences. This uses foundation models as powerful generators within an MPC framework for real-time control.
candidates.key_innovation: Merges foundation model trajectory generation with MPC for better decision-making in complex tasks, leveraging both learned preferences and model-based control.
candidates.addressed_gap: Enhances sample efficiency and long-horizon planning by incorporating foundation models into control strategies, extending MBPO's model-based ideas.

candidates.idea_title: Temporal Abstraction in Long-Horizon Preference Learning
candidates.idea_description: Use foundation models to identify key temporal segments or subgoals in trajectories, and learn preferences at multiple levels of abstraction to improve credit assignment. This involves hierarchical decomposition of trajectories for reward learning.
candidates.key_innovation: Applies temporal abstraction to preference learning, breaking down long sequences for better reward modeling by leveraging foundation models' reasoning capabilities.
candidates.addressed_gap: Addresses the challenge of credit assignment in long-horizon tasks, building on T-REX but adding abstraction to handle complexity and improve learning efficiency.

target_title: Dynamical Low-Rank Compression of Neural Networks with Robustness under Adversarial Attacks

candidates.idea_title: Dynamic Low-Rank Training with Adaptive Spectral Regularization via Reinforcement Learning
candidates.idea_description: Use reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust the condition number constraints for each layer during training based on validation performance and adversarial robustness metrics. The RL agent learns optimal regularization schedules for different low-rank cores as rank adapts.
candidates.key_innovation: Replaces static spectral regularization with an adaptive policy that learns when and how aggressively to regularize, optimizing for both accuracy and robustness jointly during rank adaptation.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current spectral regularization uses fixed condition number constraints that may not be optimal throughout the dynamic rank adaptation process, potentially under- or over-regularizing at different training stages.

candidates.idea_title: Tensor-Train Dynamical Low-Rank Approximation for Transformer Compression
candidates.idea_description: Extend dynamical low-rank training from matrix factorization to tensor-train decomposition for attention and feedforward layers in transformers. Develop projector-splitting integrators for TT-format weight evolution with spectral constraints on TT-cores.
candidates.key_innovation: Moves beyond 2D matrix factorization to higher-order tensor decompositions naturally suited for transformers' multi-dimensional weight tensors, enabling more efficient compression of modern architectures.
candidates.addressed_gap: Existing DLRA methods are primarily designed for convolutional networks with matrix-shaped weights, leaving transformers—which dominate modern deep learning—without a principled dynamic low-rank training framework.

candidates.idea_title: Certified Adversarial Robustness via Dynamically Compressed Lipschitz Networks
candidates.idea_description: Derive formal robustness certificates for dynamically low-rank trained networks by analyzing how rank adaptation affects Lipschitz constants. Develop efficient certification methods that leverage the structured sparsity of low-rank factors.
candidates.key_innovation: Provides mathematical guarantees about robustness for rank-adaptive compressed models, moving beyond empirical robustness improvements to certifiable defense against adversarial attacks.
candidates.addressed_gap: Prior work connects spectral properties to Lipschitz bounds but doesn't provide certification for dynamically compressed models where the network structure changes during training.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Layer Spectral Co-Regulation for Global Condition Number Control
candidates.idea_description: Instead of regularizing each low-rank core independently, develop a cross-layer regularization scheme that controls the overall condition number of the network's input-output Jacobian. Use the DLRA framework to jointly optimize factorizations across layers.
candidates.key_innovation: Shifts from layer-wise to network-wide spectral control, acknowledging that adversarial vulnerability depends on the composition of layers rather than individual layer conditioning.
candidates.addressed_gap: Current spectral regularizers treat each layer independently, but the overall robustness depends on how singular values propagate through the entire network.

candidates.idea_title: Physics-Informed Dynamical Low-Rank Training for Neural ODEs
candidates.idea_description: Apply dynamical low-rank approximation to compress Neural ODE networks by factorizing the weight matrices in the continuous-depth layers. Develop symplectic projector-splitting integrators that preserve energy conservation properties when applicable.
candidates.key_innovation: Bridges DLRA with continuous-depth networks, leveraging the natural connection between dynamical systems theory and Neural ODEs for structure-preserving compression.
candidates.addressed_gap: DLRA has been applied to discrete-layer networks but not to continuous-depth architectures, which have unique compression opportunities and stability considerations.

candidates.idea_title: Task-Aware Rank Adaptation via Information-Theoretic Criteria
candidates.idea_description: Develop rank adaptation criteria based on mutual information between intermediate representations and task labels, rather than just the magnitude of singular values. Use DLRA machinery to evolve factorizations that maximize task-relevant information flow.
candidates.key_innovation: Moves from purely spectral rank adaptation to information-theoretic criteria, ensuring that compression preserves task-relevant features rather than just high-energy components.
candidates.addressed_gap: Existing rank adaptation methods rely on singular value thresholds, which may discard task-relevant information that happens to have small singular values.

candidates.idea_title: Hardware-Aware Dynamic Low-Rank Training with Spectral Constraints
candidates.idea_description: Integrate hardware-specific latency and energy constraints into the DLRA training loop, jointly optimizing model accuracy, adversarial robustness, and deployment efficiency. The rank adaptation and spectral regularization become hardware-aware.
candidates.key_innovation: Explicitly models hardware constraints during training, making compression decisions that balance accuracy, robustness, and actual deployment efficiency rather than just theoretical FLOP counts.
candidates.addressed_gap: Existing methods optimize for accuracy and robustness in isolation from deployment hardware, potentially creating models that are theoretically efficient but practically suboptimal.

candidates.idea_title: Dynamic Low-Rank Training for Federated Learning with Differential Privacy
candidates.idea_description: Develop a federated DLRA framework where clients perform local low-rank training with spectral regularization, and the server aggregates factorized models while maintaining differential privacy. Use projector-splitting integrators that are noise-tolerant.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines low-rank compression, robust training, and privacy preservation in federated settings, addressing multiple deployment constraints simultaneously.
candidates.addressed_gap: Prior work treats compression, robustness, and privacy as separate problems, but real-world deployment requires all three.

candidates.idea_title: Evolutionary Strategies for Optimizing Low-Rank Core Geometries
candidates.idea_description: Use evolutionary algorithms to discover optimal factorization geometries (e.g., shared vs. layer-specific ranks, structured sparsity patterns in factors) for different network architectures and tasks, then apply DLRA within these geometries.
candidates.key_innovation: Automates the architectural design of low-rank parameterizations rather than assuming a fixed factorization structure, discovering novel compression geometries through search.
candidates.addressed_gap: Existing methods assume a standard matrix factorization without exploring whether alternative factorization geometries might be more effective for specific architectures.

candidates.idea_title: Continual Learning with Dynamical Low-Rank Expansion and Spectral Forgetting Prevention
candidates.idea_description: Use DLRA to enable efficient continual learning by expanding rank (adding new factors) for new tasks while using spectral regularization to prevent catastrophic forgetting. The condition number constraints maintain stable representations for previous tasks.
candidates.key_innovation: Unifies model compression with continual learning, using rank expansion for new knowledge and spectral constraints to preserve old knowledge in a principled way.
candidates.addressed_gap: Continual learning and model compression are typically addressed separately, but their combination offers unique efficiency and stability benefits not explored in prior work.

candidates.idea_title: Dynamic Low-Rank Adaptation with Meta-Learned Regularization Schedules
candidates.idea_description: Use meta-learning to automatically determine the strength of the condition number regularizer at each training step and layer, based on the current low-rank core statistics and training progress. This would replace the need for manually tuning the regularization hyperparameter, which is often sensitive to the architecture and dataset.
candidates.key_innovation: The regularization schedule is not fixed but learned via a meta-objective that balances accuracy, robustness, and compression efficiency during the dynamical low-rank training process.
candidates.addressed_gap: The original method uses a fixed regularization strength, which may be suboptimal and requires expensive hyperparameter tuning. This idea addresses the gap by making the regularization adaptive and data-driven.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Layer Spectral Conditioning via Global Low-Rank Core Interaction
candidates.idea_description: Instead of controlling the condition number of each low-rank core independently, enforce a global condition number constraint on the product of low-rank cores across layers. This would directly control the sensitivity of the entire network to input perturbations, leading to more certified robustness.
candidates.key_innovation: Moves from layer-wise spectral control to a network-wide conditioning constraint that accounts for inter-layer interactions, providing a tighter bound on the overall Lipschitz constant.
candidates.addressed_gap: Prior work only regularizes individual layers; this addresses the gap by considering the cumulative effect of low-rank transformations across the network, which is crucial for end-to-end robustness.

candidates.idea_title: Low-Rank Cores as Adversarial Perturbation Detectors
candidates.idea_description: Monitor the condition number of the low-rank cores during inference as a signal for detecting out-of-distribution or adversarial inputs. A sudden change in the condition number could indicate an input that is poorly represented in the low-rank manifold, triggering a rejection or a more robust evaluation.
candidates.key_innovation: Repurposes the spectral properties of the low-rank cores, which are already trained for robustness, as a built-in anomaly detector without additional modules.
candidates.addressed_gap: The existing work focuses on robustness during training but does not leverage the low-rank structure for inference-time detection. This idea addresses the gap by using the same spectral information for both defense and detection.

candidates.idea_title: Tensor-Train Dynamical Low-Rank Training for Transformers
candidates.idea_description: Extend the dynamical low-rank framework to compress and control the spectral properties of Transformer attention and feed-forward layers using tensor-train (TT) decompositions. This would exploit the multi-linear structure of Transformers for more aggressive compression while maintaining robustness via TT-core conditioning.
candidates.key_innovation: Applies dynamical low-rank training and spectral regularization to the high-dimensional weight tensors in Transformers, which are typically not addressed by layer-wise matrix decompositions.
candidates.addressed_gap: The predecessors focus on CNNs and fully connected layers; this idea addresses the gap by scaling the approach to the dominant architecture in modern deep learning.

candidates.idea_title: Rank Adaptivity via Condition Number-Driven Pruning
candidates.idea_description: Use the condition number of the low-rank cores as a criterion for rank adaptation: cores with condition numbers above a threshold are candidates for rank reduction, while those with very low condition numbers might indicate underfitting and could be increased. This ties the rank decision directly to the spectral regularizer.
candidates.key_innovation: The rank adaptation is no longer based solely on approximation error (as in DLRA) but also on the spectral properties, promoting a balance between efficiency and robustness.
candidates.addressed_gap: The original rank-adaptive mechanism in DLRA is based on the singular value decay, which may not consider robustness. This idea addresses the gap by integrating robustness into the rank decision.

candidates.idea_title: Federated Learning with Communication-Efficient Low-Rank Spectral Updates
candidates.idea_description: In federated settings, clients train low-rank models locally and send only the low-rank cores (and possibly their condition numbers) to the server for aggregation. The spectral regularizer can be applied locally to ensure robustness, and the low-rank format reduces communication overhead.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines the communication efficiency of low-rank updates with the robustness of spectral control in a federated learning framework, addressing both efficiency and privacy constraints.
candidates.addressed_gap: The existing work is centralized; this idea addresses the gap by extending the approach to distributed settings where communication efficiency and robustness are critical.

candidates.idea_title: Certified Robustness Bounds for Dynamical Low-Rank Networks
candidates.idea_description: Derive formal certified robustness guarantees for networks trained with dynamical low-rank approximation and condition number regularization. This would involve bounding the Lipschitz constant of the entire network in terms of the condition numbers of its low-rank cores and the ranks.
candidates.key_innovation: Provides theoretical guarantees that the empirical robustness gains from spectral regularization translate into provable robustness margins.
candidates.addressed_gap: The previous work only shows empirical robustness; this idea addresses the gap by offering a theoretical foundation for certified robustness in compressed low-rank models.

candidates.idea_title: Low-Rank Manifold Optimization with Natural Gradient for Cores
candidates.idea_description: Replace the projector-splitting integrator with a natural gradient method that respects the low-rank manifold geometry and incorporates the condition number regularizer. This could lead to more stable and faster convergence by accounting for the curvature of the parameter space.
candidates.key_innovation: Integrates natural gradient descent, which is known for its efficiency in high-dimensional spaces, with the dynamical low-rank framework, while explicitly maintaining spectral control.
candidates.addressed_gap: The projector-splitting integrator is efficient but does not consider the Riemannian geometry of the low-rank manifold with respect to the regularizer. This idea addresses the gap by designing an optimizer that jointly optimizes the objective and the regularization constraints.

candidates.idea_title: Continual Learning with Low-Rank Spectral Constraints
candidates.idea_description: Use dynamical low-rank training to restrict weight updates to a low-dimensional subspace for each task, and apply spectral regularization to the cores to prevent catastrophic forgetting. The low-rank manifold acts as a memory-efficient scaffold, while the condition number control ensures stability across tasks.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines the benefits of low-rank parameterization (for memory and computation) with spectral control (for stability) to address both efficiency and catastrophic forgetting in continual learning.
candidates.addressed_gap: The existing work does not consider sequential task learning; this idea addresses the gap by applying the framework to continual learning, where both compression and robustness to distribution shifts are crucial.

candidates.idea_title: Data-Dependent Low-Rank Basis via Online SVD with Spectral Regularization
candidates.idea_description: Instead of using a fixed low-rank basis (e.g., from SVD of initial weights), dynamically update the basis using online SVD or random projections on the training data, while simultaneously regularizing the condition number of the cores. This could lead to a more data-adaptive low-rank structure.
candidates.key_innovation: The low-rank basis is not static but evolves with the data distribution during training, potentially capturing more relevant features while maintaining robustness through spectral control.
candidates.addressed_gap: The standard DLRA uses a fixed low-rank basis from initialization; this idea addresses the gap by making the basis data-dependent, which may improve compression and robustness in non-stationary environments.

candidates.idea_title: Dynamical Rank Adaptation via Gradient Curvature Analysis
candidates.idea_description: This method dynamically adjusts the rank of each layer's low-rank factorization during training by analyzing the curvature of the loss function using Hessian approximations or gradient variance. It enables more efficient parameter allocation where the model is most sensitive, improving convergence and final accuracy.
candidates.key_innovation: Integrates DLRA with second-order optimization insights for intelligent rank scheduling, moving beyond fixed or heuristic rank adjustments to a data-driven, adaptive approach.
candidates.addressed_gap: The predecessors use DLRA but do not adapt rank based on optimization dynamics; this addresses the limitation of static rank settings by responding to training progress.

candidates.idea_title: Low-Rank Attention with Condition Number Regularization in Transformers
candidates.idea_description: Apply dynamical low-rank approximation to transformer attention layers, coupling it with condition number regularization to control singular values of attention weights. This compresses transformers while enhancing adversarial robustness through spectral control.
candidates.key_innovation: Extends DLRA and spectral control to modern transformer architectures, combining efficiency and robustness in a context not covered by predecessors focused on CNNs.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors lack application to transformers; this addresses the gap by adapting the techniques to a dominant architecture, enabling scalable and robust models.

candidates.idea_title: Joint Low-Rank and Quantization for Certified Robust Compression
candidates.idea_description: Combine dynamical low-rank training with quantization, where low-rank cores are quantized and spectral regularization ensures bounded Lipschitz constants for certified robustness. This yields models that are both compressed and provably robust to perturbations.
candidates.key_innovation: Merges two compression techniques (low-rank and quantization) with robustness guarantees, addressing multiple efficiency and safety constraints in a unified framework.
candidates.addressed_gap: Previous work often treats low-rank and quantization separately; this integrates them with spectral control for synergistic benefits in compression and robustness.

candidates.idea_title: Federated Low-Rank Learning with Spectral Privacy Control
candidates.idea_description: Use DLRA for efficient communication in federated learning, where clients update low-rank factors locally, and add spectral regularization to control condition numbers to prevent model inversion attacks, enhancing privacy.
candidates.key_innovation: Applies DLRA and spectral control to distributed settings, focusing on communication efficiency and privacy preservation, which are critical in federated contexts.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors do not consider federated learning or privacy; this addresses scalability and security gaps in modern learning paradigms.

candidates.idea_title: Neural Architecture Search with Embedded Condition Number Constraints
candidates.idea_description: Integrate condition number regularization into neural architecture search (NAS) to discover architectures that are inherently robust and efficient. The search space includes low-rank parameterizations, and the objective balances accuracy, robustness, and compression metrics.
candidates.key_innovation: Embeds spectral control into NAS, automating the design of models that are optimized for both efficiency and adversarial robustness from the ground up.
candidates.addressed_gap: NAS typically optimizes for accuracy and model size, not robustness; this adds a new dimension to architecture design inspired by spectral principles.

candidates.idea_title: Dynamical Low-Rank for Continual Learning with Forgetting Mitigation
candidates.idea_description: Apply DLRA to continual learning, where low-rank factors are evolved across tasks while regularization controls condition numbers to preserve knowledge and reduce catastrophic forgetting. The low-rank structure acts as a structured memory for task consolidation.
candidates.key_innovation: Uses DLRA as a mechanism for continual learning, leveraging its adaptive, low-rank manifold to manage sequential updates without erasing prior information.
candidates.addressed_gap: Continual learning suffers from forgetting; this addresses it by providing a principled, spectral-aware update rule based on low-rank dynamics.

candidates.idea_title: Condition Number-Regularized GANs for Stable and Robust Generation
candidates.idea_description: Apply spectral regularization to the discriminator in generative adversarial networks (GANs), using low-rank approximations to control singular values and improve training stability. This could reduce mode collapse and enhance robustness to adversarial examples.
candidates.key_innovation: Extends spectral control to generative models, addressing GAN-specific issues like instability, which are not covered in predecessors focused on discriminative tasks.
candidates.addressed_gap: Predecessors focus on classification models; this applies the ideas to generative AI, improving reliability in applications like image synthesis.

candidates.idea_title: Integrated Adversarial Training within Dynamical Low-Rank Optimization
candidates.idea_description: Incorporate adversarial example generation directly into the DLRA training loop, where low-rank cores are updated to minimize adversarial loss simultaneously with clean loss. This ensures compressed models are inherently robust from the start of training.
candidates.key_innovation: Combines adversarial training with DLRA in an end-to-end manner, rather than applying robustness as a post-hoc regularization, for more effective defense.
candidates.addressed_gap: The predecessors use spectral regularization for robustness but not explicit adversarial training during low-rank optimization; this integrates both for stronger guarantees.

candidates.idea_title: Energy-Aware Low-Rank Training via Spectral Efficiency Metrics
candidates.idea_description: Define energy efficiency metrics based on the condition numbers of low-rank cores, and use DLRA to optimize for minimal energy consumption during inference and training. This involves hardware-aware regularizers that link spectral properties to computational costs.
candidates.key_innovation: Focuses on energy efficiency as a key constraint, using spectral analysis to reduce power usage, which is a practical concern in AI deployment.
candidates.addressed_gap: Efficiency in predecessors is about parameters and FLOPs, not energy; this addresses a new practical dimension for sustainable AI systems.

candidates.idea_title: Condition Number-Guided Pruning in Dynamical Low-Rank Networks
candidates.idea_description: Use the condition numbers of low-rank cores to guide pruning decisions, where elements with high sensitivity based on singular values are pruned first. This combines pruning with low-rank approximation for superior compression while maintaining robustness.
candidates.key_innovation: Integrates pruning with spectral analysis within DLRA, creating a principled compression method that leverages condition numbers for accuracy-aware sparsity.
candidates.addressed_gap: Pruning and low-rank approximation are often applied separately; this synergizes them using spectral information to improve compression ratios and robustness.

target_title: A Clean Slate for Offline Reinforcement Learning

candidates.idea_title: Dynamic Policy Constraint Adjustment via Uncertainty-Calibrated Penalties
candidates.idea_description: Develop an algorithm that adaptively modulates policy constraint strength (e.g., divergence penalty in BEAR) based on real-time uncertainty estimates from ensemble Q-functions, allowing tighter constraints in high-uncertainty data regions and relaxed constraints where value estimates are reliable. This is implemented within the clean-slate framework to isolate the effect of adaptive constraints.
candidates.key_innovation: Moves beyond static, fixed constraint coefficients in BCQ/BEAR by dynamically adjusting constraint strength per state-action pair based on learned uncertainty, enabling more nuanced offline learning.
candidates.addressed_gap: The predecessor taxonomy treats policy constraints as static; this addresses the limitation that fixed constraints may be overly restrictive in data-rich regions and insufficiently conservative in sparse regions, which can lead to suboptimal policy support.

candidates.idea_title: Cross-Domain Offline RL Transfer with Clean-Slate Implementations
